signal_event.title: Responses API adds compaction and computer environment
signal_event.summary: Demo source covering agent runtime improvements, compaction, and computer environment support as a reusable foundation for production agents.

why_it_matters: Compaction is becoming a baseline primitive for long-running agents.
A richer runtime lowers the amount of bespoke orchestration each product has to maintain.
recommended_actions: Refresh HoluBot memory and context notes.
Draft one public-safe architecture brief while the topic window is still open.

signal_event.title: Agent interoperability keeps converging around MCP style contracts
signal_event.summary: Demo source describing a convergence trend around shared tool contracts, context exchange, and agent-to-agent interoperability.

why_it_matters: Protocol convergence can shrink the integration tax across future agent products.
It creates a clearer opportunity window for sidecar products that sit beside existing assistants.
recommended_actions: Keep HoluBot sidecar-friendly and avoid deep chat-chain coupling.
Track which protocol assumptions should become reusable shared core modules.

signal_event.title: Coding workflows keep stretching context and review loops
signal_event.summary: Demo source focused on coding-agent workflow upgrades, longer context handling, and review-oriented execution patterns.

why_it_matters: Longer context only matters when paired with stable review and iteration loops.
This is a content and product cue for practical engineering workflows, not just bigger numbers.
recommended_actions: Capture one brief on stable coding loops instead of raw context-window marketing.
Compare which workflow assumptions should move into the sidecar backlog.
